Title: Identification and molecular characterization of the nicotianamine synthase gene family in bread wheat

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Fe and other divalent metals for metal uptake, transport and homeostasis; serves as precursor of mugineic acid family phytosiderophores involved in Fe acquisition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to nicotianamine biosynthesis in bread wheat; likely supports Fe homeostasis and Fe-related nutrient transport
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved YXXΦ and di-leucine motifs; short C-terminal region
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nicotianamine biosynthesis / mugineic acid family phytosiderophore precursor pathway / iron homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: NA biosynthesis supplying precursor pool for deoxymugineic acid and metal chelation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; supplies precursor pool for mugineic acid family phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous expression across many tissues and developmental stages in Chinese Spring; no expression of homeolog TaNAS1-B detected in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in most tissue types and developmental stages in Chinese Spring; clade I genes overall expressed in roots during vegetative growth, but specific Fe-deficiency response for TaNAS1-A not individually highlighted
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron sufficiency versus iron deficiency in hydroponic culture
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene identification and sequencing; phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR tissue profiling; qRT-PCR under Fe deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS1-A is one of 21 wheat NAS genes identified and belongs to clade I subgroup 1. 2. It shows broad expression across tissues and developmental stages. 3. As a NAS enzyme, it is inferred to synthesize nicotianamine, supporting Fe transport/homeostasis and precursor supply for phytosiderophore biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly tested; altered NAS expression may modify Fe deficiency responses and adaptation to Fe-rich or Fe-poor soils
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TaNAS1-A protein contains characteristic NAS superfamily endocytosis motifs and a short C-terminal region similar to barley HvNAS5-1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving Fe homeostasis and grain nutritional quality through modulation of nicotianamine production, though gene-specific deployment was not tested

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Fe and other divalent metals for transport and homeostasis; precursor of mugineic acid family phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes nicotianamine synthase but no expression was detected in Chinese Spring or Gladius in the tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with YXXΦ and di-leucine motifs; short C-terminal region
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; supplies precursor for phytosiderophore biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: No expression detected in Chinese Spring; no expression detected in Gladius root/shoot under tested Fe conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron sufficiency versus iron deficiency in hydroponic culture
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene identification and sequencing; phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS1-B is a clade I subgroup 1 NAS homeolog. 2. It encodes a typical NAS family protein but transcript was not detected in tested materials/conditions. 3. It remains a potential contributor to nicotianamine biosynthesis depending on genotype or condition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Short C-terminal region similar to TaNAS1-A
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential reservoir allele for Fe nutrition traits, but functional deployment requires confirmation of expression/activity

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal chelation, Fe transport and homeostasis, precursor of mugineic acid family phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nicotianamine biosynthesis in roots, leaves and developing grain tissues, potentially supporting Fe mobilization and grain loading
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with N-terminal secretory signal peptide and NAS endocytosis motifs
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nicotianamine biosynthesis / phytosiderophore precursor pathway / iron homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: NA biosynthesis for internal chelation and precursor supply to DMA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; supplies precursor pool for mugineic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in young root tissues, seedling leaf and reproductive organs; highest in caryopsis 3–5 DAP in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots under vegetative growth; clade I genes are generally root expressed and Fe deficiency inducible
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron sufficiency versus iron deficiency; developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene sequencing; phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR tissue profiling; qRT-PCR Fe treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS2-A belongs to clade I subgroup 2 and encodes a typical NAS enzyme. 2. It is strongly expressed in young roots but also in leaves and reproductive tissues, with maximal expression in caryopsis 3–5 DAP. 3. It likely contributes to both vegetative Fe homeostasis and developing grain Fe mobilization through nicotianamine production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered expression may modify adaptation to Fe-rich/Fe-poor soils; specific penalties not tested
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presence of secretory signal peptide suggests possible atypical subcellular routing relative to other NAS proteins
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for biofortification because of expression in developing grain and likely role in bioavailable Fe accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal chelation and precursor for phytosiderophore biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putative NAS enzyme, but no expression detected in Chinese Spring or Gladius under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: No expression detected in Chinese Spring or Gladius under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS2-D1 is a clade I subgroup 2 NAS homeolog. 2. It was identified genomically but transcript was not detected in the tested tissues/conditions. 3. Its metabolic role remains inferred from sequence homology rather than expression evidence in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely arose from wheat-specific duplication within the TaNAS2 group
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low immediate value until expression/function are validated

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Fe and other metals and supplies precursor for phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nicotianamine biosynthesis in early root tissues; likely supports root Fe uptake and transport
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved NAS motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Root NA production feeding metal chelation and likely DMA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; supplies precursor pool for DMA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed in embryonic radicle 2 DAS and seedling root 10–12 DAS in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No expression detected in Gladius root/shoot under Fe treatment conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage; iron sufficiency versus deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS2-D2 is highly expressed in early root tissues during germination/seedling growth. 2. It likely contributes to root nicotianamine production for Fe handling at early developmental stages. 3. Expression was not detected in vegetative-stage Gladius Fe-treatment experiments, indicating genotype- or stage-specific expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homeolog-specific expression divergence is evident within the TaNAS2 group
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for improving early seedling Fe acquisition if stage-specific expression can be leveraged

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Fe/Zn and supports metal homeostasis; precursor for phytosiderophore biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports nicotianamine production in early root tissues and is part of the Fe-deficiency-inducible clade I root NAS program
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved YXXΦ and LL motifs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; supports precursor supply for DMA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in embryonic radicle 2 DAS and seedling root 10–12 DAS in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: As one of the 14 clade I genes, root expression is strongly induced by Fe deficiency in Gladius
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron deficiency in roots; early germination and seedling stages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene sequencing; qRT-PCR tissue profiling; qRT-PCR Fe treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS3-A is highly expressed in radicle and seedling root. 2. It belongs to the clade I set strongly up-regulated in roots by Fe deficiency. 3. It likely contributes to enhanced root nicotianamine and downstream phytosiderophore-related Fe acquisition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Putative D-genome homeolog was only partially recovered as TaNAS8-U
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for improving Fe deficiency tolerance via enhanced root Fe acquisition chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal chelation and precursor supply for phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports root nicotianamine biosynthesis during early growth and under Fe deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved motifs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; supplies precursor for DMA branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in embryonic radicle 2 DAS and seedling root 10–12 DAS in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Likely among the Fe-deficiency-induced clade I root genes in Gladius
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene sequencing; qRT-PCR tissue profiling; qRT-PCR Fe treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS3-B mirrors TaNAS3-A with strong expression in radicle and seedling root. 2. It is part of the clade I root-biased NAS gene set associated with Fe deficiency response. 3. It likely increases nicotianamine availability for root Fe acquisition and mobilization.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents wheat-specific expansion relative to barley synteny
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate allele for engineering stronger root Fe-deficiency response

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal transport/homeostasis and precursor for phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to leaf-associated nicotianamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved NAS motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed in leaf tissues in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Part of clade I genes expressed in roots and induced by Fe deficiency in Gladius, though tissue profile emphasizes leaf expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS4-A is a clade I subgroup 3 NAS gene. 2. It is most highly expressed in leaf tissues in Chinese Spring. 3. It likely supports shoot metal chelation/homeostasis through nicotianamine synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homeology with TaNAS4-D is influenced by known 4AL-5AL translocation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving shoot Fe distribution and grain loading indirectly

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal homeostasis and precursor for phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putative NAS enzyme present in Gladius but not expressed/detected in Chinese Spring; cultivar polymorphism suggests variable contribution to nicotianamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Could only be amplified from Gladius DNA; no expression detected in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No expression detected in Chinese Spring; genotype dependent
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS4-U likely represents the B-genome homeolog of TaNAS4. 2. It shows strong cultivar dependence, being amplified only from Gladius genomic DNA. 3. Its contribution to nicotianamine metabolism appears genotype specific.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: May be cultivar-specific or highly polymorphic between Gladius and Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as natural variation for breeding Fe-homeostasis traits if functional alleles segregate among cultivars

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports metal homeostasis and phytosiderophore precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to leaf-associated nicotianamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed in leaf tissues in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Likely among root-expressed clade I genes in Gladius Fe experiment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS4-D is a TaNAS4 homeolog with strongest expression in leaves. 2. It likely contributes to shoot nicotianamine production and Fe distribution. 3. Along with TaNAS4-A it reflects wheat chromosomal rearrangement history while retaining NAS function.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Separated from TaNAS4-A by known 4AL-5AL translocation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modulating shoot Fe transport/homeostasis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal chelation/homeostasis and precursor for phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes modestly to nicotianamine production in developing grain; potential functionality differs between cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with predicted mitochondrial targeting peptide and conserved NAS motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Generally low expression with highest levels in caryopsis 3–5 DAP; functional in Gladius but Chinese Spring contains a premature stop codon
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene sequencing; qRT-PCR; sequence comparison showing premature stop codon in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS5-B is a unique B-genome NAS gene without A/D homeologs. 2. It shows low expression, enriched in caryopsis 3–5 DAP, and has predicted mitochondrial targeting. 3. Chinese Spring carries a premature stop codon, indicating cultivar-dependent functionality.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Natural loss-of-function in some cultivars may alter Fe homeostasis or grain loading, but consequences were not directly measured
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rapid evolution/cultivar polymorphism highlighted
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Interesting natural variant for association studies on grain Fe nutrition and organellar Fe homeostasis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal chelation/homeostasis and precursor to phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: caryopsis 3–5 DAP as inferred from expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely supports nicotianamine biosynthesis at low levels, particularly in developing caryopsis; may function in chloroplast-associated metal homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with chloroplast transit peptide, conserved YXXΦ and LL motifs, and short C-terminal region
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Generally low expression with most expression in caryopsis 3–5 DAP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Part of clade I genes likely induced in roots under Fe deficiency, though baseline tissue profile is low
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene sequencing; in silico targeting analysis; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS6-A has a chloroplast transit peptide and atypical protein architecture. 2. It is weakly expressed overall, with relatively higher expression in caryopsis 3–5 DAP. 3. It may contribute to chloroplast or grain nicotianamine pools involved in Fe homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the NAS proteins proposed to support organellar Fe homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential niche target for organellar Fe homeostasis and grain Fe biofortification

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal homeostasis and precursor to phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: caryopsis 3–5 DAP as inferred from expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Low-level contributor to nicotianamine biosynthesis, enriched in developing caryopsis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Generally low expression with most expression in caryopsis 3–5 DAP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Likely part of the Fe-deficiency-responsive clade I root genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS6-B is a low-expression clade I NAS gene. 2. Its highest expression occurs in caryopsis 3–5 DAP. 3. It likely contributes modestly to grain-associated nicotianamine production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homeolog of TaNAS6-A and TaNAS6-D
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for grain Fe biofortification

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal homeostasis and precursor to phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: caryopsis 3–5 DAP as inferred from expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to nicotianamine biosynthesis in developing grain and possibly chloroplast-associated Fe homeostasis; expressed more strongly than A/B homeologs
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with chloroplast transit peptide and conserved NAS motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low overall expression with most expression in caryopsis 3–5 DAP; about threefold higher than TaNAS6-A and TaNAS6-B
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Likely part of Fe-deficiency-induced clade I root set in Gladius
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene sequencing; EST support for N-terminus; in silico targeting; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS6-D encodes a chloroplast-targeted NAS-like protein with unusual long N-terminus. 2. It is expressed at low levels but is the strongest-expressed TaNAS6 homeolog, especially in caryopsis 3–5 DAP. 3. It may contribute to organellar and grain nicotianamine-mediated Fe homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: An EST supports transcription of its long N-terminal transit peptide region
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising homeolog within the TaNAS6 group for targeting grain and organellar Fe management

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal transport/homeostasis and precursor to phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: embryonic radicle and seedling root as inferred from expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports root nicotianamine biosynthesis during early growth and under Fe deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved motifs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; contributes precursor for DMA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed in embryonic radicle 2 DAS and seedling root 10–12 DAS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: As clade I root NAS gene, likely induced by Fe deficiency in Gladius roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS7-A1 is root-biased during early growth. 2. It likely contributes to nicotianamine pools used for Fe mobilization and phytosiderophore synthesis. 3. It is part of the clade I NAS system induced in roots under Fe deficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely originated from wheat-specific duplication/translocation history
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enhancing seedling/root Fe acquisition

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Fe and other metals; likely protects reproductive tissues against oxidative damage and supports metal delivery
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely drives strong nicotianamine biosynthesis in anthers, supporting reproductive Fe homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly anther-specific expression, about 100-fold higher in anther than all other organs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS7-A2 shows extremely strong anther-specific expression. 2. It likely produces nicotianamine for Fe chelation/protection in reproductive tissues. 3. The study highlights wheat anthers as major Fe sinks linked to TaNAS7-A2 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as potentially important for protecting anthers against oxidative damage through Fe chelation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for maintaining fertility and reproductive Fe homeostasis; caution needed if altering expression due to likely reproductive specificity

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal homeostasis and precursor to phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: embryonic radicle and seedling root as inferred from expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports nicotianamine biosynthesis in early root tissues and Fe deficiency response
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAS family protein with conserved motifs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; precursor supply for DMA branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed in embryonic radicle 2 DAS and seedling root 10–12 DAS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: As one of the clade I genes, likely induced in roots under Fe deficiency in Gladius
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS7-D is strongly expressed in early root tissues. 2. It likely contributes to root nicotianamine and downstream Fe-acquisition chemistry. 3. It groups with clade I genes associated with Fe-deficiency-responsive root expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous/syntenic relationship to barley HvNAS4/HvNAS7 duplication pair discussed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for strengthening root Fe deficiency responses

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal homeostasis and precursor to phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Partial NAS family sequence
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted NAS activity based on homology
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous expression detected in most tissues and developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Partial sequence identification; qRT-PCR tissue profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS8-U is only partially sequenced and likely corresponds to the missing D-genome homeolog of TaNAS3. 2. It shows broad expression across tissues. 3. Functional inference as a NAS gene is based on sequence similarity and expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only 168 bp partial sequence obtained
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Requires full-length validation before practical use

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal chelation/homeostasis, particularly in shoots; precursor to phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely major clade II contributor to shoot/leaf nicotianamine biosynthesis; expression in roots under Fe deficiency is low and unclear
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Clade II NAS family protein with conserved motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed in leaf tissues; TaNAS9-A expression is about 10-fold higher than TaNAS9-B and TaNAS9-D
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed at low levels in roots under Fe deficiency; also among few genes expressed in shoots at very low level
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene sequencing; phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR tissue profiling and Fe treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS9-A is a clade II NAS gene with strong leaf-biased expression. 2. Its transcript level exceeds those of the B and D homeologs in shoots. 3. Clade II genes appear to contribute more to shoot-specific nicotianamine production than clade I genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Root Fe-deficiency response was low and pattern unclear compared with clade I genes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Attractive target for manipulating shoot Fe distribution and potentially grain loading without primarily altering root Fe-acquisition machinery

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal chelation/homeostasis, especially in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to leaf-associated nicotianamine biosynthesis as a clade II homeolog
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Clade II NAS family protein
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed in leaf tissues but lower than TaNAS9-A
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed at low levels in shoots of Gladius; Fe deficiency tends to reduce shoot expression overall
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron sufficiency versus deficiency; developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS9-B is a leaf-biased clade II NAS homeolog. 2. It likely contributes to shoot nicotianamine production. 3. Compared with clade I genes, clade II genes are more associated with shoot expression patterns.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the few TaNAS genes expressed in Gladius shoots
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supporting target for improving shoot Fe distribution

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal chelation/homeostasis in shoots and precursor to phytosiderophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to leaf nicotianamine synthesis; root Fe-deficiency response is low compared with clade I genes
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Clade II NAS family protein
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed in leaf tissues in Chinese Spring
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed at low levels in roots under Fe deficiency in Gladius with no clear pattern; among the few genes detectable in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAS9-D is a clade II NAS homeolog with leaf-biased expression. 2. It shows only weak/unclear root response to Fe deficiency compared with clade I genes. 3. It likely functions mainly in shoot nicotianamine metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Clade II genes are orthologous to barley NASHOR2 and rice OsNAS3
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May help refine shoot Fe homeostasis traits in breeding
